title: Market Brief — Wednesday, July 1, 2026
summary: Next-gen compute is severely tight: H200 util-avail sits at just 5% and B200 at 7%, signaling near-total absorption of frontier silicon. Legacy hardware is looser with H100 at 27% and A100 at 32%. The biggest daily move was H200 spot surging 30.5% to $4.08/hr, confirming demand is outrunning supply on the chips that actually matter for large-model inference.
paragraphs: Next-gen compute is severely tight: H200 util-avail sits at just 5% and B200 at 7%, signaling near-total absorption of frontier silicon. Legacy hardware is looser with H100 at 27% and A100 at 32%. The biggest daily move was H200 spot surging 30.5% to $4.08/hr, confirming demand is outrunning supply on the chips that actually matter for large-model inference.

At TCO-adjusted costs (1.25x spot), B200 inference runs $4.80/M tokens for Llama 70B, making medium-tier pricing a modest loss; H100 and H200 operators selling at medium rates lose $9.06/M and $12.76/M respectively. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.34/M output is burning cash on any GPU class. OpenAI's o3 Pro at $80/M output prints money. Grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

H200 and B200 compute is undervalued given single-digit availability and explosive utilization gains of over 40pp in 30 days, making reserved capacity on these chips the clearest long trade. OpenAI's premium reasoning tier at $80/M output is overvalued versus Anthropic Opus 4.6 at $25/M with comparable capability, so shift reasoning workloads to Anthropic and pocket the spread.
bullets: 5% util-avail on H200 marks the tightest compute class in the stack, deep in severe-shortage territory.
93.1% B200 utilization today reflects a 43.4pp surge over 30 days, the fastest ramp across all GPU classes.
$4.08/hr H200 spot jumped 30.5% day-over-day, the largest single-session move in the dataset.
$0.34/M output DeepSeek V3.2 sits well below the cheapest TCO-adjusted production cost of $4.80/M on B200.
21.36 LLMflation index means frontier token prices have fallen roughly 79% since GPT-4 launch.
$0.15/M output from open-source models like Qwen3-30B now undercuts every major proprietary vendor.
